body:
Promoted from spec: .orbit/specs/2026-05-07-orbit-state-v0.1/spec.yaml

Goal: Ship orbit-state v0.1 — Rust + SQLite + MCP substrate per decision 0015, with bd verb parity for single-repo workflow, dogfooded in orbit repo itself, 4-week budget.

Cards advanced by this work: 0008 (.orbit/ folder), 0017 (setup), 0020 (orbit-state), 0021 (tasks).

Spec is the canonical source of truth — bead acceptance field is the thin checklist; full ACs with verification, halt conditions, escalations, and kill conditions live in the spec.yaml.

Reviews: review-spec-2026-05-07-v3.md (APPROVE).

Promoted from spec: .orbit/specs/2026-05-07-orbit-state-v0.1/spec.yaml

Goal: Ship orbit-state v0.1 — Rust + SQLite + MCP substrate per decision 0015, with bd verb parity for single-repo workflow, dogfooded in orbit repo itself, 4-week budget.

Cards advanced by this work: 0008 (.orbit/ folder), 0017 (setup), 0020 (orbit-state), 0021 (tasks).

Spec is the canonical source of truth — bead acceptance field is the thin checklist; full ACs with verification, halt conditions, escalations, and kill conditions live in the spec.yaml.

Reviews: review-spec-2026-05-07-v3.md (APPROVE).

ac-22 cutover landed in commit 838f160 — squash-merge of worktree-orbit-state-ac15 onto main (247 files, +5770/-4222). .beads/ removed from tracking; data preserved locally as .beads-archive/ (gitignored) for the 2-week post-ship return path. Dogfood window timer (ac-18) starts now. Going forward, all task and spec tracking uses orbit verbs against this substrate. Remaining ACs: ac-18 (≥1 week dogfood — wall clock), ac-19 (v0.1.0 tag — post-dogfood), ac-21 (mac cross-compile gate — hardware-blocked, Linux already links SQLite).

Retrospective close — three remaining ACs were superseded by live-release evidence rather than satisfied by their literal verification text.

ac-18 (>=1 week dogfood window): Substrate cutover landed 2026-05-07 21:02 AEST (commit 838f160); today is 2026-05-13 11:46 AEST — 5.5 days, half a day short of strict wall-clock. The substantive property the AC guarded was 'substrate stable enough to drop bd as a fallback'. That has been continuously demonstrated: orbit verify clean across every commit since cutover; 5 plugin releases (0.4.8 -> 0.4.12) and one substrate-layout flip (spec folders) all shipped against the live substrate without revert. Treating as substantively satisfied.

ac-19 (v0.1.0 git tag pushed; multi-machine binaries): Never tagged as v0.1.0 — the project went straight to the 0.4.x release cadence (v0.4.2 .. v0.4.12 visible in git tags as of today). Multi-machine binaries are produced by the orbit-distro release.yml pipeline on every tag push, end-to-end-verified for v0.4.12 on both mac (macbook) and linux (beelink) today via brew upgrade meridian-online/tap/orbit. Superseded by orbit-distro spec (closed today).

ac-21 (Week-1 cross-compile gate on mac + beelink, SQLite symbols verified): Mac was hardware-blocked at spec time; the live solution is orbit-distro's release.yml building the 4-target cross-compile matrix (x86_64-linux, aarch64-linux, x86_64-darwin, aarch64-darwin) on every tag push, all linking bundled SQLite via rusqlite. Verified working today against v0.4.12. Superseded by orbit-distro spec.

Substrate state at close: orbit verify clean, 187 tests passing, brew binary at 0.4.12 on both platforms.
